headline: Data Center Demand Suddenly in Doubt
detail: A key narrative shift is questioning whether AI infrastructure spending will stay as robust as priced in. This hits AVGO directly — it derives massive revenue from custom AI chips and data center networking. CRWD also lives in enterprise tech spend, which softens if data center budgets tighten.

headline: South Korea: Samsung and SK Hynix Expand AI/Chip Mega-Projects
detail: Big investment commitments from Korean chipmakers signal continued long-term semiconductor demand, which is a tailwind for SMH. For your book this is mild positive color on the sector, though it doesn't fix near-term price action in AVGO.

headline: U.S.-Iran Pause Hostilities, Hormuz Shipping Resumes
detail: The de-escalation removes an acute tail risk that was rattling equity futures over the weekend. Calmer oil markets support a steadier broad tape, which gives your SPY and QQQ positions a modest near-term reprieve but doesn't change your individual stock problems.

headline: China Widens Export Curbs Targeting Defense and Tech Firms
detail: China expanding export restrictions keeps geopolitical supply-chain risk alive for the semiconductor sector. SMH and AVGO are exposed if restrictions broaden further into chip-related materials or equipment — worth watching for escalation.
